mission.title: opentofu: Direct support for conditional single-instance resources
mission.description: Direct support for conditional single-instance resources. Requested by 139+ users.

steps.title: Check current opentofu deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentofu version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
helm list -n opentofu 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentofu installation.

steps.title: Review opentofu configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentofu configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
kubectl get configmap -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentofu
```
### OpenTofu Version

```shell
None. Currently using Hashicorp Terraform.
```

### Use Cases

We often use `count = var.xxxx ? 1 : 0` in various flavours but with the same purpose of determining to create or not create a single resource.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Direct support for conditional single-instance resources
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
kubectl get events -n opentofu --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Direct support for conditional single-instance resources" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This is just a basic prototype of supporting a configuration like the following:

```hcl
terraform {
  # The "enabled" argument currently requires opting in to this experiment,
  # because adding a new meta-argument is a breaking change and we've
  # not yet decided how to handle that.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentofu installed or the issue environment reproducible.
